config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name of the switch. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the sensor in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Address
config_entries.description: start address of the first register in a range (can be decimal or hexadecimal).

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the switch. See [https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/switch/#device-class](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/switch/#device-class) for a list of available options.

config_entries.label: Restore Mode
config_entries.description: Control how the switch attempts to restore state on bootup. **NOTE** : Not all components consider **restore_mode**. Check the documentation of the specific component to understand how this feature works for a particular component or device. For restoring on ESP8266s, also see `restore_from_flash` in the [esp8266 section](https://esphome.io/components/esp8266/).

options.label: Attempt to restore state and default to OFF if not possible to restore.
Attempt to restore state and default to ON.
Always initialize the switch as ON on bootup.
Attempt to restore state inverted from the previous state and default to OFF.
Attempt to restore state inverted from the previous state and default to ON.

Does nothing and leaves it up to the downstream platform component to decide. For example, the component could read hardware and determine the state, or have a specific configuration option to regulate initial state.

config_entries.label: Inverted
config_entries.description: Whether to invert the binary state, i.e. report ON states as OFF and vice versa. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Assumed State
config_entries.description: Disables updates (periodic read commands) for this register. Default is false.

config_entries.label: Bitmask
config_entries.description: Some values are packed in a response. The bitmask is used to determined if the result is true or false. See Bitmasks.

config_entries.label: Custom Command
config_entries.description: raw bytes for modbus command. This allows using non-standard commands. If custom_command is used address and register_type can't be used. Custom data must contain all required bytes including the modbus device address. The CRC is automatically calculated and appended to the command. See Using custom_command how to use custom_command.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Lambda
config_entries.description: Lambda to be evaluated every update interval to read the status of the switch.

config_entries.label: Offset
config_entries.description: Offset from start address in bytes (only required for uncommon response encodings). If more than one register is written in a command, this value is used to find the start of this datapoint relative to the start address. The component calculates the size of the range based on offset and size of the value type. The value for offset depends on the register type. For holding input registers, the offset is in bytes. For coil and discrete input resisters, the LSB of the first data byte contains the coil addressed in the request. The other coils follow toward the high-order end of this byte and from low order to high order in subsequent bytes. For registers, the offset is the position of the relevant bit. To get the value of the coil register, 2 can be retrieved using address: 2 / offset: 0 or address: 0 / offset 2.

config_entries.label: Skip Updates
config_entries.description: By default, all sensors of a modbus_controller are updated together. For data points that don't change very frequently, updates can be skipped. A value of 5 would only update this sensor range in every 5th update cycle. Note: The modbus_controller groups components by address ranges to reduce number of transactions. All components with the same starting address will be updated in one request. skip_updates applies for all components in the same range.

config_entries.label: Use Write Multiple
config_entries.description: By default the modbus command Function Code 6 (Preset Single Registers) is used for setting the holding register if only one register is set. If your device only supports Function Code 16 (Preset Multiple Registers) set this option to true.

config_entries.label: Write Lambda
config_entries.description: Lambda called before send. Lambda is evaluated before the modbus write command is created. Parameters passed into the lambda note: because the response contains data for all registers in the same range you have to use data[item->offset] to get the first response byte for your sensor. Possible return values for the lambda.
